overall_summary: The ElizaOS ecosystem is navigating a period of high community tension regarding token utility and migration transparency, despite the beta release of the Milady app and successful infrastructure integrations with Babylon and decentralized identity protocols.
key_facts: Odilitime announced the Milady beta release on March 21, 2026, following the resolution of several build fixes.
The Babylon integration was confirmed to include a community airdrop and a token buyback mechanism for ElizaCloud.
ElizaOS 1.x CLI and ElizaCloud users reported critical disk image upload failures and missing Discord plugin modules.
A new permissionless on-chain raffle plugin, Moltraffle, was released for the Base blockchain utilizing Chainlink VRF.
Development of the SAID Protocol was initiated to provide agents with verifiable digital identities on the Solana blockchain.
The token migration concluded with 1 billion tokens minted, while 40% of the community allocation remains a point of contention among holders.
Integrations for WhatsApp, Gmail, and the N8N workflow engine were finalized to expand agent reach across third-party platforms.
open_questions: By when will team mint remaining tokens and finish selling? Will project shut down after team sells all tokens?
What is one concrete use case of this token?
Will the project keep being built if the token goes to 1M market cap?
Is there a way to reload the container through the Eliza Cloud GUI?
How do you setup a coin faucet into a website?

new_issues_prs.title: Major database refactor to improve architecture
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Foundational change for system performance and long-term reliability.

new_issues_prs.title: SAID Protocol for on-chain Solana identity
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Enables verifiable agent signatures and decentralized identity.

overall_focus.claim: Focus shifted toward official beta launch stability, clearing functional blockers, and refining AI personality for engagement.

discord_updates.summary: Intense debate regarding token price performance (-84% for many), lack of utility, and perceived poor communication from leadership.
Clarification of ecosystem hierarchy: ElizaOS is the base OS; Milady is built on top of it; OpenClaw agents are compatible within Milady.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Community members demand actual token utility tied to products rather than just 'memecoin' narratives.
Frustration over deployment errors in Eliza Cloud, specifically regarding missing Discord plugin modules and long Docker build times.

strategic_insights.insight: While the team provides daily and weekly updates, they are failing to address the specific economic concerns of token holders, leading to a perception of 'shadow' abandonment.
implications_or_questions: Does the current communication strategy need a pivot from technical updates to economic roadmap updates?

strategic_insights.insight: The explicit positioning of ElizaOS as a foundational 'Operating System' for other apps like Milady and OpenClaw protects it from being replaced by internal spin-offs.
implications_or_questions: How can this modularity be better marketed to third-party developers?

market_analysis.observation: Token price recovery is lagging behind the broader market, even post-Babylon launch.
market_analysis.relevance: Identified by the team as a sign that 'something isn't right,' potentially requiring CEX listings or perps to resolve liquidity issues.
